description_long: This cowshed is a rectangular, single-story agricultural building with a steeply pitched, red hip roof made of corrugated material. The exterior walls are constructed from horizontal wooden planks, giving it a rustic and sturdy appearance. Along the longer sides, there are large, dark metal hinges and latches, suggesting the presence of wide barn-style doors for livestock or equipment access. The structure sits on a slightly raised stone or concrete foundation, providing durability and protection from ground moisture. There are no visible windows, but the eaves are open, likely for passive ventilation. The overall design is functional, with minimal decorative elements, emphasizing practicality and ease of maintenance.
description: A rectangular wooden cowshed with a red hip roof, large barn doors, and open eaves for ventilation. The structure is robust and utilitarian, with no visible windows.
description_view_0: Side view showing the long wooden wall with two large barn doors and visible metal hinges under the red hip roof.
description_view_1: End view displaying the triangular gable end of the roof and a plain wooden wall with no doors or windows.
description_view_2: Opposite side view similar to view 0, but without visible doors, only the wooden wall and roof.
description_view_3: Rear view showing a plain wooden wall beneath the hip roof, with open eaves visible at the top.
attribute_door_style: double sliding barn door, wooden with metal hinges and latches
